cooperates.cooperate_desc: Khufra can easily lock down enemies which can make them easy targets for Granger. Granger's long ult range compliments Khufra's long 1st skill range which means that there's always a follow up for every set you make if you have a Granger in your team.
CC monster duo that has very high reach, versatility, mobility, and sustain. Petrify also helps with locking down enemies so Zhuxin can stack and lift them up for more CC and dmg!

Khufra can easily lock down enemies which can make them easy targets for Kimmy. A bully duo, especially with the petrify. Make the enemies feel more of Kimmy's dmg which Khufra's CC chain and anti-escape capabilities.

counters.counter_desc: Against very good Joy users, Khufra is hard to utilize. Joy's CC Immunity at full stacks makes her escape Khufra's CC and move around freely while doing heavy mgc dmg. Very deadly for the team and unstoppable in most cases.

Khufra can't deal with many interruptions, especially Fredrinn's. If only Fredrinn can't cancel the Bouncing Ball, he wouldn't be too much of a problem, but he can. A bully to Khufra in my opinion. Despite that, there are of course work arounds which can be learned through experience.

A general counter to CC dependent initiators. My priority ban because you can't initiate properly in the game if Diggie is in the enemy. Very annoying dmg, and can even interrupt your 1st skill with Diggie's 2nd skill. Just ban it HAHA!
A counter-engage tank is Khufra's weakness, but there are work arounds of course. Thos depends on the situation, and relies on deep game knowledge and experience

dominants.dominant_desc: At the start of the game, get the 1st skill and help your mid-laner to clear as fast as possible. Forget dueling and aim your first skill to the minions and trigger Concussive Blast to clear and rotate to gold lane with your mid-laner immediately to gank and get the early first blood and give your team an edge. I've won soo many games doing this with my mid-laner and having the upper hand early on.

Before the turtle spawns, assist in the exp lane to secure a fast clear with the exp laner and get in position. You're doing this so your exp laner can front while you're waiting for your lvl 4 (because that will be the most case scenario). And with enough time, you should be able to get your ult and use it to stun and control the enemy jungler.

In the later stages of the game, many players forget that no man is an island. You can immediately initiate when the enemy's roamer get out of position and you have enough teammates to back you up to take down the roamer or the cocky exp laner of the enemy. Choose your battles, and remember that nmhigher numbers win!

This is how you tower dive very aggressively with Khufra. This one is very risky, but very worth it if you done it right. You'll position yourself behind the bush in the tower. Yes, the enemy will see you coming there and they won't expect it. Your high mobility will compensate for that vision, and you'll be in position when you needed. Charge the 1st skill when you get to the bush, and get that tower dive. This cuts off the target's escape route and guarantees that they will come to you. Bonus tip: they often become chickens and just press themselves to the wall where the tower is which makes them very easy targets when you're in this very uncomfortable position, easy aim and easy ult!

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem set prioritizes Movement Spd. The idea is making Khufra be able rotate on the map faster than the opponent's roamer to position, initiate, and bait out skills because of high mobility. You won't expect a bouncing ball to move soo freely don't you? Next thing they know is that they've missed their skills because you're a hard target to hit because of speed. Also very good for chasing and helps your passive's heal!

equips.equip_desc: Your only non-negotiables here are the first 3 items. Choose High HP items because that compliments Khufra's Skills. Before, I used to include Cursed Helmet instead of Guardian Helmet but because of the update on Guardian Helmet, it provides more sustainability as it inherited Twilight Armor's passive.

This build is generally good against well-spreaded physical attack enemy composition. If you're expecting more skill-based PHY dmg, use this build. I suggest building Dreadnought armor before purchasing Chastise Pauldron first and proceed as follows.

This gives more defense and sustain if you're up against heroes that heavily relies on basic attacks. Specifically designed if you're up against heroes like Lesley, Irithel, Ling, Obsidia, Minsitthar, Aulus, etc.

hero.hero_overview: If you like to constantly annoy enemies and stop them on their tracks, Khufra's unique skill set is just for you. He's not a very popular pick, but people underestimates what he can do and dominates even if he's considered Off-META! I value your search for greatness so READ and LIKE for more insightfule content!
hero.hero_strength: Khufra is an initiator-type tank. Excells in ambushing, crowd control, chasing enemies. One of the mobile tanks out there that can rotate fast, and support in teamfights almost every time. Best in annoying enemies with his unique ability (2nd skill - Bouncing Ball) that stops dashing enemies. Great counter for assasins and stunning priority targets before the fight even starts.
hero.hero_weakness: Weak against heroes that gains CC Immunity. Highly skill-dependent so landing skill shots is crucial. Also weak against heroes that can interrupt 2nd skill and those that aren't affected by it.

data.recommend: Unlock your next tank from the best. Introducing my peculiar Khufra!

spell.spell_desc:
For this guide, we'll use Khufra more aggressively, sacrificing range for additional CC. Forget flicker tricks and being fancy because Petrify offers low risk, high reward plays. Perfect for Off-META and unexpected plays! This spell pairs very well with all of his skills:
(1st - petri -- for initiating)
(2nd - petri -- for chasing)
(petri - Ult -- for secured sets).

data.title: Crushing in Off-META with Khufra!

data.title: Khufra god

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Khufra pairs well with Guinevere as he can send enemies airborne, thus allowing the latter more avenues of dmg.

counters.counter_desc: Khufra may be fast, but he can't heal as fast in prolonged combat. Joy can quickly close the distance and easily keep up Khufra's mobility and dmg him in turn by forcing combat time to extend and not allowing him to heal.

dominants.dominant_desc: By this stage, you need to time your skill casts and achieve consecutive control over your opponents, allowing your team the advantage in fights. Use his passive as a ranged finisher if you find yourself chasing a low hp enemy.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Agility builds off his rapid repositioning, allowing him further response capabilities to roam lanes

Festival of Blood allows him to regen mid combat, as his passive allows him to heal from range, though it's usually used as a poke-heal strat. 

Concussive Blast is the bread and butter of heroes like Khufra that require the tank to stick close to enemies rather than take in the damage up front like a shield. It doubles as an AoE DMG source.

equips.equip_desc:
Speed, defense and regeneration are key to his continued survival. 

The effects of Thunder Belt and Cursed Helmet give him a debuff support for his allies, allowing them to deal more damage or to keep other heroes from getting away. 

War Axe, is a decent damage source and increases his regen capabilities, but is replaceable depending on the situation at hand.

hero.hero_overview: Khufra is great at being either an initiator or a tank. Both are on the premise that the player is tactical about their use of Khufra's skillset to quickly get close to enemies and stall them for their teammate to DPS or to finish off a runner by themselves with a burst.
hero.hero_strength:
Rapid repositioning. 
High Mobility capacity in bursts.
Quick healing when proficient in his skills
hero.hero_weakness:
DPS alone is mediocre.
Even with his high mobility, Khufra suffers from those that do not know when to retreat or to wait for his passive to ready itself. 
Greatly weakened by slowness as he cannot tank as much damage in long sustained combat.

data.recommend: Khufra demands a sense of the battlefield, primarily when to engage and when to retreat. Know your distances.

spell.spell_desc: Greatly enhances survival in fights, especially against those with high DPS or atk spd.

data.title: Dbs

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Pharsa: When you lock them down, she rains destruction from above.
Granger: Kufra's CC gives Granger free shots with no escape for the enemy.
Lylia: The enemy is trapped? Lylia's bombs say hello.

counters.counter_desc: Valir's knockback keeps Kufra from getting close. His constant poke and CC make it hard for Kufra to engage cleanly.
Kimmy's long-range poke means Kufra can't safely approach. She can kite him all day and never get close enough for him to CC her.
Diggie's ultimate completely shuts down Kufra's crowd control. When Diggie is around, your combos feel like throwing a party where no one shows up.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game:
Focus on vision and protecting your jungle. Use 1st skill to block invades or surprise ganks. Don't waste your 2nd skill randomly - save it to stop enemy dashes.

Mid Game:
Kufra becomes a team fight king. Hide in bushes, predict enemy dashes, and lock down high-value targets.

Late Game:
Stick with your team. A good Kufra engage can decide the game. Watch out for Purify - that spell can ruin your ultimate!

emblems.emblem_desc:
Tank Emblem - Brave Smite
Brave Smite keeps you healthy while you dive into the enemy team. The more you CC, the more you heal.

Other good emblem talents:

Tenacity: Boosts your defense when your HP is low - perfect for those "I'm not dead yet!" moments.

Firmness: Extra physical defense early to survive lane poke and jungle invades.

equips.equip_desc:
Tough Boots / Warrior Boots: Depending on the enemy team, get magic or physical defense early.

Cursed Helmet: Helps you deal a bit of damage while tanking minions and jungle monsters during rotations.

Antique Cuirass: For dealing with marksmen and fighters - cuts down their damage the longer they hit you.

Athena's Shield: Against magic burst heroes like Eudora or Xavier - because getting one-shot isn't fun.

Dominance Ice: Slows enemy attack speed and regen -goodbye, lifesteal heroes.

Immortality: Because no one expects Kufra to come back for round two.

Optional: Radiant Armor if facing magic DPS like Valir or Chang'e.

hero.hero_overview:
Kufra is a tank hero feared for his powerful crowd control and ability to shut down mobile enemies. With his bouncing ball and terrifying ultimate, Kufra can disrupt entire enemy teams and stop dash-happy assassins in their tracks. He shines in team fights and is the nightmare of any hero who relies on mobility to survive.

Use Kufra to control the battlefield like a bouncer at the door - no dashes allowed, and if they try, they get slammed into the wall.
hero.hero_strength:
Excellent crowd control that can lock down multiple enemies

Perfect counter to dash-heavy heroes (goodbye Ling, Lancelot, and Wanwan)

Strong initiator for team fights

His second skill gives him great durability by reducing damage taken

Can set up game-changing combos with his ultimate
hero.hero_weakness:
No real damage - if your team doesn't follow up, you're just delaying your own death

Struggles against heroes who can poke from long range

Needs precise positioning - a whiffed combo means a wasted opportunity

Relies on teammates to capitalize on his crowd control

Tip: Kufra stops dashes... but sadly, he can't stop bad teammates. Ping that map and set up those ambushes!

data.recommend: Kufra Guide: Master Crowd Control and Anti-Dash Tactics to Dominate Every Team Fight - The Ultimate Tank for Mobile Legends Warriors

spell.spell_desc: Flicker lets Kufra make surprise plays by suddenly engaging from unexpected angles. Combine Flicker with your 2nd skill or ultimate to catch enemies off guard - nothing like making an assassin regret jumping in!

data.title: Kufra Guide

dominants.dominant_desc: Inicia ayudando al jg, sin esperar que finalize su buff, ataca al mago esto le dará ventaja al tuyo y mejora su rotación, ya después ataca al adc enemigo si está un poco adelante de la tarre pégale de sorpresa le darás ventaja a tú adc y si el adc es activo puede ser una kill casi segura, revisa si el jg enemigo esta en el bicho cercano ya debe estar tu jg Serca fastidiar siempre y retroceder, estás iniciando no aguantas demasiado, pero ellos tampoco
dominants.dominant_title: Para iniciar lo ideal es que ayudes un poco al jg, no esperar que eliminé al buff atacar al mago para darle ventaja al tuyoy luego al adc enemigo, si tú adc te apoya puede ser una kill

emblems.emblem_desc: Ya a ser un tank con aguacate y daño le conviene un equipo full tank le sirve para aguantar daño y molestar al enemigo

equips.equip_desc: Botas duras con golpe terrible sirve para disminuir el cc en el y general daño al enemigo, si intentan escapar sirve para finalizar con el daño de las del golpe terrible, cinturón su escalado combina muy bien con su pasiva ya que carga muy rápido las carga dándole una gran cantidad de aguante en late corta cura un itens esencial para toda las partidas, su función es disminuir la curación al enemigo y el dps de ellos, casco malditos combina perfecto ya que es un tank que inicia y este pegado al enemigo funciona perfecto, también para limpiar los Minions si tú tean no está cerca, alas de la reina, para aguantar y más daño, con este equipo ya puedes eliminar a un mago o adc de un combo si no se an equipado aguante, lo final sería coraza, crepúsculo, radiante o Atenea, esto depende del enemigo, lo que que moleste más le metes tu defensa

hero.hero_overview: Este es un tank con cc regeneración y daño es gran iniciador, de los mejores tank del momento si se sabe aprovechar sus abilidades
hero.hero_strength: Es un tank muy versátil sirve en toda la partida casi por igual, como tank hay que tener en cuenta que es para cuidar a tu tean y no entrar a matar, siempre priorizar la visión del mapa y no mostrarse mucho ya que pierde el elemento sorpresa, ya para iniciar puedes utilizar para porque, ya que con su primera y segunda puedes entrar y salir de una pelea sin mucha complicaciones, no usar su útil al menos que tenga a alguien de tu equipo Serca, como dato en la te con 4 equipos dependiendo del enemigo el kufra se puede eliminar a un mago adc o jg de un combo completo sin ayuda de su equipo, eso sí el enemigo no compra ningún artículo de defensa
hero.hero_weakness: Es débil contra enemigo con mucha realentisacion y regeneración ya que es un héroe que depende mucho de su movilidad y cc, héroes como valir, matirda, florín, shusin, son una gran molestia para este tank

dominants.dominant_desc: These are some tips and tricks for the hero Kharfra... Never rush the first skill, take your time, and also you have to extend the first skill to the required distance only. Do not use the first skill higher than the required distance, otherwise you will lose control. There are also some strategies. One of these strategies is if the hero Fanny is against you, you have to open the second skill at the beginning of the match because the team members may attack you at the beginning of the match, so opening the second skill is necessary. And if the hero Fanny is not against you, you have to help the magician by cleaning up the minions and helping the path that is weak at the beginning of the match.

emblems.emblem_desc: I chose these features and these emblems because they give the hero good defense and movement speed as well as quickly cleaning up minions to help the mage reach the warrior and archer lines.

equips.equip_desc: I chose this build because it gives the hero physical and magical defense at the same time, with damage tolerance and movement speed as well.
I chose this build because it gives the hero increased physical and magical defense over time and this build can be useful for matches that last for a long time.

hero.hero_overview: Hero Khafre Background: - He is an armored hero who is characterized by high durability and tolerance to damage due to the second ability that increases his physical and magical defense... The hero's role in the game: - The role of the hero Khafre in the game is to defend the team members by receiving damage and also stopping the heroes who are characterized by high movement speed and also starting the fight... Farming methods for the hero Khafre: - By purchasing the wandering boots and he will get gold over time from the team members
hero.hero_strength: Advantages of the roaming and farming path for the hero Khafre: - The invisibility advantages can be purchased for roaming because they are very useful with this hero by obtaining movement speed and invisibility for you and the team members, and the farming advantages for this hero are concerned with helping the team and obtaining gold from assistance and from killing the team for the minions // Advantages of the early game for the hero: - His defense is relatively good for the beginning of the match after opening the second level and the ability to control the enemy heroes with high and slow movement as well, and he is very annoying at the end of the match due to his high control ability and excellent defense. The hero Khafre is distinguished by his distinctive ability that gives him the ability to cause damage to enemies and slow them down slightly, and also restore life points after hitting the special skill using the normal attack
hero.hero_weakness: Disadvantages of the passages and farming for the hero Khafre: - There are no disadvantages in the passages for the hero Khafre nor in farming as well, as he is a balanced hero in this regard // Disadvantages of team fighting: - There are not many disadvantages in team fighting except for the first skill, which takes time to use again if you make a mistake in catching the enemy, then here you either lose your life or lose some of the team members // Disadvantages of the game (early-medium-late) There are no disadvantages due to his increasing defense with the length of the match due to purchasing equipment and the second skill that increases his physical and magical defense

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Ruby is a perfect match for Khufra, when Ruby's ult get a at least 3 heroes, Khufra can use his First skill to jump at the enemy and use Ult to them and it's a perfect chain CC.

counters.counter_desc: Diggie is a huge counter for Khufra as of why, because one Ult from Diggie and your effort to CC the enemies will be in vain and can turn the tide of a teamfight.

dominants.dominant_desc: You can hide in Bushes and charge his First skill and wait for the perfect timing to jump at enemies and you can also use it to check bushes and you're lucky if it has enemies into it. This is good for positioning before a Turtle Fight and Lord Dance.
dominants.dominant_title: In the early game, Khufra can use his First skjll to suprise the enemy and give your teammates to deal DMG to the enemy.

emblems.emblem_desc: Tank Emblem is the best Emblem for Khufra because of the extra HP it gives and Hybrid DEF. And Firmness is perfect for Khufra because of the extra Hybrid DEF, Wilderness Blessing is good for faster rotation between lanes and bushes. Lastly, Concussive Blast, it's perfect for khufra because of the additional HP Magic Damage to nearby enemies.

equips.equip_desc: Tough Boots for anti-CC, Thunder Belt because of it's HP, Hybrid DEF and additional Movement SPD it gives, Dominance Ice to counter ATK SPD based heroes, Antique Cuirass to counter Burst Physical heroes, Athena's Shield to counter Burst Magic Herors. Lastly, Immortality for late game.

hero.hero_overview: Khufra is a Roamer Hero and a Tank, as a Roamer you must buy Roam boots at early. And you must follow and protect you Gold Laner as they'll have a huge impact in mid-to-late game.
hero.hero_strength: Khufra is strong against dash heroes such as; Fanny, Harith, Joy and etc. Khufra is also good at CC because of his Charged First skill and the dash cancelation of his Second skill, lastly his Ultimate.
hero.hero_weakness: Khufe is weak against Diggie and Purify.

data.recommend: Jumper Man(Khufra) Guide for all the Players that wants to try Jumper Man.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is the most effective Battle Spell, with Flicker you can use it in various situations, such as the Ult + Flicker Combo and First skill and then Flicker. You can also use Flicker to chase or back out.

data.title: Jumper Man Guide

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Khufra's skills + Faramis' 2nd skill ensures the enemies exhaust their blink/mobility skills. Your team mates will  have a good time picking off enemy heroes after they use up their escape skills. Faramis can also extend team fights with his Ultimate.
4 disabling skills in total can be made if playing with Kadita, ensuring enemies are unable to react to the burst combo that this mage has.

counters.counter_desc: Diggie can counter all of Khufra's skills with his Ultimate(Time's Journey)
While Valentina is weak against Khufra's 2nd skill, she can copy Khufra's Ultimate and use it against your team.
Popol's traps will disrupt your plan to attack. It will make you immobile once triggered and it provides indefinite vision which will make your surprise attacks very predictable.

dominants.dominant_desc: Buy Boots with Conceal Blessing. Leash your jungler until your Concussive Blast is triggered. Immediately provide vision by visiting enemy's Orange or Purple buff locations. Notify your jungler where the enemy jungler is. Once done, provide support to your jungler by providing vision and control in the Lithowanderer area. After the Lithowanderer contention, let your Midlaner visit the Exp Lane so you would get to Level 4 quicker and clear 2 waves in the midlane.

The 1st Turtle should spawn by now. By this time, you should already be or close to being at Level 4. Ensure that you are visible to the enemies for them to feel intimidated but not at risk of you being picked off. Use your 1st skill to check bushes and reserve your 2nd skill to fend off possible steals from the enemy jungler. If your team gets the turtle, there is a high chance your team will call a pick off. Target the squishy enemy heroes first (marksman, mage, jungler).

By this time you should have 2 key items (Thunder Belt with stacks, Dominance Ice or Athena's Shield). You should be able to sustain damage from the enemies. Prioritize hiding in the bushes nearest the enemy base. Ready your 1st skill for possible pick off. 2nd skill should be reserved for enemy heroes who can steal the Lord or initiate team fights. If enemies arent visible, you or the Exp Laner should check the bushes around the Lord Pit as the enemies could be hiding in one of those.

You can support your team's marksman or jungler if the situation sees fit. The best strategy is for you, the midlaner and the jungler roam around the map to take potential kills and deny the enemy jungler's Orange or Purple buffs.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Agility for faster map rotation.
Tenacity to sustain incoming damage. 
Concussive blast for passive damage together with Thunderbelt.

equips.equip_desc:
Tough Boots for magic defense and lower CC time;   
Conceal Blessing for surprise attacks team mobility.
Dominance Ice for slowing down attack speed and minimizing enemy spell vamp.
Thunder Belt for tankiness and to supplement the damage from Concussive Blast.
Queen's Wings for extra damage reduction and cooldown.
Athena's Shield for magic damage reduction.
Immortality for extended sustain in team fights.

Tough Boots for magic defense and lower CC time;   
Conceal Blessing for surprise attacks team mobility.
Athena's Shield for magic damage reduction.
Thunder Belt for tankiness and to supplement the damage from Concussive Blast.
Cursed Helmet for addiotional HP-based passive damage and magic damage reduction.
Guardian Helmet for sustain and to boost passive damage from Concussive Blast, Thunder Belt and Cursed Helmet.
Immortality for extended sustain in team fights.

Conceal Blessing for surprise attacks team mobility.
Dominance Ice for slowing down attack speed and minimizing enemy spell vamp.
Thunder Belt for tankiness and to supplement the damage from Concussive Blast.
Antique Cuirass to greatly lessen enemy's physical damage.
Queen's Wings for extra damage reduction and cooldown.
Guardian Helmet for sustain and to boost passive damage from Concussive Blast and Thunder Belt

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Thanks to her wide range ultimate, Pharsha can help Khufra effectively and safely.
Having a lot of blink skills that are also wide, will be very helpful when Kufra starts initiation
Thanks to her wide range ultimate, Ixia can help Khufra effectively and safely.
Thanks to her wide range ultimate, Beatrix can help Khufra effectively and safely.

counters.counter_desc: Diggie's ultimate can remove CC effects
Akai's Ultimate can turn things around when Kufra starts the initiation
Not only does it have high burst damage, it can also avoid Kufra's initiation (if the user's fingers are fast enough).
Can push and cause slow effects, ultimate removes CC, painful poke damage

dominants.dominant_desc: Buy roam items and help the jungler farm for a few seconds. Then go to the river area to open a path or help the midlaner. Go to the lane area of the team that is having trouble fighting their enemies. Ask the midlaner to let us clear 1 wave of their minions, this is done so that we get level 4 and prepare for the first Turtle.
As a tank roamer, it is important to always adjust items to the enemy. So that team fights are more effective and can provide debuff effects to enemies.

emblems.emblem_desc: Tank and HP regen

equips.equip_desc: We can use Encourage or Conseal as roam items (If the game enters the late game, it is recommended to use Conseal for better initiation). We can use Kufra's passive to trigger Thunderbelt's passive, with that we can collect Thunderbelt passive stacks safely.

hero.hero_strength: High defense stats, one of the best initiator, prevents enemies from using blink skills with his second skill
hero.hero_weakness: Diggie, Zhuxin, Valir
